trades.catalyst: March CPI release April 10 showing weaker than expected energy/shelter inflation
trades.risk_notes: Energy prices could spike further; shelter inflation sticky

trades.catalyst: Sticky inflation readings keeping Fed hawkish through 2026
trades.risk_notes: Economic slowdown could force cuts; geopolitical shocks

trades.catalyst: Base effects and seasonal patterns favor moderate March reading
trades.risk_notes: Energy disruptions from Iran conflict; unexpected shelter surge

trades.catalyst: Atlanta Fed GDPNow showing 1.6% Q1 growth, well below 2.0% threshold
trades.risk_notes: BEA revisions could be more positive than nowcasts suggest

avoid.reason: Spread too wide (>10%); insufficient edge given uncertainty
Already resolved/about to resolve with obvious outcomes priced at 99%
Spread >5% and insufficient crypto market data for credible edge

avoid.market: High rate cut count markets (10+ cuts)
avoid.reason: Illiquid with 0% volume_24h; extreme tail scenarios

notes: <cite index='5-13,7-1,16-4'>Markets are pricing in a sustained inflationary regime with CME FedWatch showing 94.8% probability of April rate hold and prediction markets expecting March CPI between 3.2%-3.4% YoY versus current 2.4%</cite>. <cite index='18-1,27-3'>Cleveland Fed nowcasting 3% March CPI while Atlanta Fed GDPNow revised Q1 growth down to 1.6%</cite>, suggesting stagflationary pressures. Energy disruptions from Iran conflict creating volatility but base effects may limit March CPI upside.
